The reason is correct that red light has a longer wavelength, but the assertion that it is more energetic is wrong. Hence A is false while R is true. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/science/color/The-visible-spectrum?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Radio waves bend in a magnetic field.
Reason (R): Radio waves are electromagnetic in nature.

options.a: Both A and R are individually true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are individually true but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Radio waves propagate independently of external static magnetic fields; their paths are not bent merely by placing them in a magnetic field. The reason that radio waves are electromagnetic is true, but that fact does not cause bending; therefore the assertion is false and the reason true. ([physics.stackexchange.com](https://physics.stackexchange.com/questions/127098/do-radio-waves-interact-with-electrical-wiring?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Assertion (A): The Congress Ministries in all the provinces resigned in the year 1939.
Reason (R): The Congress did not accept the decision of the Viceroy to declare war against Germany in the context of the Second World War.

options.a: Both A and R are individually true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are individually true but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

questions.subject: Modern History
questions.explanation: Both statements are historically accurate: on 22–23 October 1939 all Congress‐ruled provincial ministries resigned, and the immediate trigger was the Viceroy’s unilateral decision (3 September 1939) to commit India to war against Germany without consulting Indian leaders. Hence R correctly explains A. ([drishtiias.com](https://www.drishtiias.com/print-quiz/3263?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Among the following, which one lays eggs and does not produce young ones directly?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The echidna (spiny anteater) is one of only two living monotremes and reproduces by laying eggs, unlike kangaroos, porcupines or whales which are viviparous mammals. ([biljoissac.wordpress.com](https://biljoissac.wordpress.com/2008/11/20/prelims-2008-answers/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: The release of which one of the following into ponds and wells helps in controlling the mosquitoes?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Gambusia (mosquitofish) are routinely introduced into wells and ponds because each fish can consume hundreds of mosquito larvae daily, making them an effective eco-friendly larvicide; crabs, dogfish and snails do not serve this purpose. ([drishtiias.com](https://www.drishtiias.com/current-affairs-news-analysis-editorials/news-analysis/20-10-2023/print/manual?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Constitution of India contains special provisions for the administration and control of Scheduled Areas in several States?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule (Articles 244 & 339) contains the special administrative provisions for Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in states other than the north-east; the Third, Seventh and Ninth Schedules deal with different matters. ([houseofupsc.com](https://houseofupsc.com/upsc-prelims-2025-pyq-fifth-schedule-scheduled-areas/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Department of Border Management is a Department of which one of the following Union Ministries?

options.c: Ministry of Shipping, Road Transport and Highways

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: Since its creation in January 2004, the Department of Border Management functions under the Ministry of Home Affairs, overseeing land and coastal border infrastructure and coordination; it is not part of Defence, Transport or Environment ministries. ([edukemy.com](https://edukemy.com/blog/upsc-prelims-topic-wise-questions-general-knowledge-important-institutions-and-agency/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: For which one of the following reforms was a Commission set up under the Chairmanship of Veerappa Moily by the Government of India?
questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.explanation: The Government of India constituted the Second Administrative Reforms Commission in August 2005 with M. Veerappa Moily as its Chairman to recommend measures for overhauling public administration; hence the Moily Commission deals with administrative reforms, not police, tax, or technical-education reforms. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Administrative_Reforms_Commission?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Elizabeth Hawley is well-known for her writings relating to which one of the following?
questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.explanation: American journalist Elizabeth Hawley gained renown for meticulously documenting mountaineering climbs in Nepal and maintaining ‘The Himalayan Database’, making her writings an authoritative record on Himalayan expeditions, not on Indian monuments, dances or wildlife. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Hawley?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In India, how many States share the coastline?
questions.subject: Geography
questions.explanation: India’s mainland coastline touches nine states—Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha and West Bengal—so the correct count is 9. Options giving 7, 8 or 10 are therefore incorrect. ([ndtv.com](https://www.ndtv.com/webstories/feature/indian-states-located-on-the-coastline-28930?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Nobel Prize–winning scientist James D. Watson is known for his work in which area?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: James D. Watson shared the 1962 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for discovering the double-helix structure of DNA, a landmark in genetics; he is not associated with metallurgy, meteorology or environmental-protection research. ([nobelprize.org](https://www.nobelprize.org/laureate/373?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:

List-I (Board)
A. Coffee Board
B. Rubber Board
C. Tea Board
D. Tobacco Board

List-II (Headquarters)
1. Bengaluru
2. Guntur
3. Kottayam
4. Kolkata

Code:
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: Coffee Board is headquartered in Bengaluru, the Rubber Board in Kottayam (Kerala), the Tea Board in Kolkata, and the Tobacco Board in Guntur (Andhra Pradesh); the only option matching A-1, B-3, C-4, D-2 is (b). ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coffee_Board_of_India?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Which one among the following has the maximum number of National Parks?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: As of 2008 Andaman & Nicobar Islands had nine notified national parks (e.g., Mahatma Gandhi Marine, Mount Harriet, Campbell Bay), whereas Arunachal Pradesh had 2, Assam 5 and Meghalaya 2. Hence it tops the list. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-2008-solved-question-paper/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: Among the following, which one is not an ape?
questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.explanation: Gibbons, gorillas and orangutans belong to the super-family Hominoidea (apes) which lack tails. Langurs are Old-World monkeys (family Cercopithecidae) and therefore are not apes. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-2008-solved-question-paper/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In how many different ways can all of 5 identical balls be placed in the cells shown above such that each row contains at least 1 ball?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Treat the 3×3 grid row-wise. Case 1: one row gets 3 balls and the other two get 1 each → 3 C 1 × 3 C 3 × (3 C 1)² = 27 ways. Case 2: one row gets 1 ball and the other two get 2 each → 3 C 1 × 3 C 1 × (3 C 2)² = 81 ways. Total = 27 + 81 = 108. ([edurev.in](https://edurev.in/t/203243/Permutation--Combination--Probability-Solved-Quest?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: There are 6 different letters and 6 correspondingly addressed envelopes. If the letters are randomly put in the envelopes, what is the probability that exactly 5 letters go into the correctly addressed envelopes?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: If exactly five letters were in the correct envelopes, the sixth envelope would automatically receive its own correct letter, making all six correct. Therefore the event “exactly five correct” is impossible and its probability is zero. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-2008-solved-question-paper/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: There are two identical red, two identical black and two identical white balls. In how many different ways can the balls be placed in the cells (each cell to contain one ball) shown above such that balls of the same colour do not occupy any two consecutive cells?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Count permissible 6-letter strings (RR, BB, WW identical pairs) with no same-colour adjacency. Starting with any colour gives 8 valid arrangements; since there are 3 choices for the starting colour, total ways = 8 × 3 = 24. ([sarthaks.com](https://www.sarthaks.com/71042/there-are-two-identical-red-two-identical-black-two-identical-white-balls-many-different?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: How many different triangles are there in the figure shown above?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: By systematically enumerating every possible triangle in the composite figure—small basic triangles, combinations that span two or more elementary triangles, and the largest outer triangle—the total comes to 28 distinct triangles. Sources that solved the same UPSC 2008 problem confirm the count as 28, making option (a) correct. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-2008-solved-question-paper/))
